exit_criteria: Knowledge captures accepted architecture decisions from the feedback build.
Docs distinguish target paths from current frontmatter code_paths so generated state stays green until implementation moves files.
Next loop receives clear planning inputs for source layout/tool lexicon migration.

loop_start.reason: User explicitly asked current session to compile accepted knowledge; feedback build captures source intent.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Documentation validation should independently check docs, generated state, and stale terminology before planning consumes the build.

next_loop.reason: Planning compiler should consume this documentation build and current KB files, not chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Scripts are disposable developer helpers and must not contain or enforce authoritative CodeWiki product policy; source, tests, and gateways must not depend on scripts.
Bootstrap behavior is owned by the CodeWiki skill workflow or a bootstrap sub-skill; source may execute it through application tools, but skill assets own prompts/templates/workflow guidance.
Prompt templates are skill assets, not arbitrary package source assets.
The web surface is a UI under src/ui, not an agent harness adapter under src/adapters.

Domain concepts such as task, roadmap, session queue, build, validation, and state belong in src/domain; application contains compilers, gateways, state engine, and agent-facing tools that use domain concepts.
Remove public project lexicon around claim; use session queue and scoped leases under session/queue instead. Legacy codewiki_claim may remain only as a transitional adapter alias until migration.
Application should expose internal agent-callable tools in src/application/tools so Pi tools, skill helpers, CLI, or future adapters call the same use-case API.

Use application/compilers for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation compilers; keep validation gateways separate under application/gateways by profile unless later evidence favors colocating them.
Use domain/state and application/state-engine naming; graph is generated representation, not the main domain concept.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Source-owned semantics and script demotion documented.
evidence_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/system/file-structure.md, .codewiki/kb/system/audits.md, .codewiki/kb/system/overview.md, and .codewiki/kb/system/alignment-model.md document scripts as optional helpers and source-owned checks/audits.
.codewiki/kb/system/file-structure.md, .codewiki/kb/system/adapters.md, and .codewiki/kb/system/extension.md define skill assets for bootstrap and prompts while source calls application tools.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Web UI ownership documented without breaking current path lint.
evidence_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/system/control-room-ui.md and product UI docs state target source belongs under src/ui/web; frontmatter code_paths retain current src/adapters/web until implementation migrates files, keeping generated state green.
.codewiki/kb/system/file-structure.md defines domain task/roadmap/session/build/validation/state and application compilers/gateways/state-engine/tools; component/data diagrams reflect the same model.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Session queue scoped lease vocabulary documented.
evidence_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/lexicon.md, .codewiki/kb/system/roadmap.md, .codewiki/kb/system/api.md, and UI docs use session queue/scoped lease language, with legacy claim naming marked transitional only.

agent_assessment: Docs now encode the target architecture while avoiding a false claim that target folders already exist. Frontmatter code_paths intentionally reference current existing implementation paths until planning/implementation moves files. This avoids generated-state warnings while giving planning a concrete migration target.
summary: Documented accepted domain-led CodeWiki refactor: source-owned semantics instead of scripts, skill-owned bootstrap/prompt assets, web UI as UI, domain/session/state vocabulary, application compilers/gateways/state-engine/tools, and session queue scoped leases.
knowledge_changes: Documented domain-led target layout in .codewiki/kb/system/file-structure.md and .codewiki/kb/system/overview.md.
Documented application tools API, compilers, separate gateways, and state-engine naming in API/compiler/gateway/graph docs.
Documented scripts as optional developer helpers, not authoritative semantics, in file-structure/audits/overview/alignment docs.
Documented skill-owned bootstrap/prompts/templates in file-structure/adapters/extension docs.
Documented web as UI target under src/ui/web while keeping current code_paths until implementation migration.
Replaced project claim vocabulary with session queue scoped leases across lexicon, roadmap, API, UI, and product docs.
Updated component/data/context diagrams and architecture diagram labels for CodeWiki UI, application tools, domain concepts, state engine, and scoped leases.
roadmap_changes: No direct roadmap mutation; next planning layer should refine or create tasks for source layout migration and compatibility strategy.
assumptions: Documentation build is allowed to change product/system UI/user docs because public vocabulary and UI source ownership affect product surfaces too.
Implementation migration will be planned separately from this documentation build.
open_questions: Exact cutover strategy for legacy codewiki_claim tool name remains a planning decision.
Exact order for moving src/adapters/web to src/ui/web versus adapter launch wrappers remains a planning decision.
